settings.title: I Start With Understanding. The Camera Comes Second.

settings.editor:
Before I shoot anything, I need to know three things: who you actually are, who you want to reach, and what it is about you that will make those people stop and pay attention.

That's not a casual chat over coffee. That's the whole job. Everything else, the light, the timing, the location, the moment I decide to press the shutter, all flow from that understanding.

Technically, I've spent 15+ years making the craft invisible. The technical side is the foundation. What I bring beyond it is the ability to see what makes you genuinely you, and find the precise moment, light, and angle where that becomes an image someone can't scroll past.
